Apple Scab.symptoms: Dark olive-green spots on leaves, velvety texture, leaf curling and premature drop

treatment.immediate_action: Remove and destroy fallen infected leaves. Prune affected branches for better air circulation.
treatment.pesticide: Mancozeb 75WP (Dithane M-45) at 2.5g per litre of water OR Carbendazim 50WP (Bavistin) at 1g per litre
treatment.application: Spray at 10-14 day intervals starting from bud break. Ensure full coverage of leaf surfaces.
treatment.prevention: Plant resistant varieties like Liberty or Enterprise. Maintain proper spacing between trees. Apply dormant season copper spray.

Apple Black Rot.symptoms: Brown circular lesions with concentric rings on fruit, frogeye leaf spots

treatment.immediate_action: Remove mummified fruits and cankers from trees immediately. Prune dead wood.
treatment.pesticide: Captan 50WP at 2.5g per litre OR Thiophanate-methyl (Topsin-M) at 1g per litre
treatment.application: Begin spraying at green tip stage. Repeat every 10 days through petal fall.
treatment.prevention: Remove all dead wood during winter pruning. Destroy fallen fruit. Maintain orchard hygiene.

Apple Cedar Rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface, tube-like projections on leaf undersides

treatment.immediate_action: Remove nearby juniper or cedar trees if possible as they are alternate hosts.
treatment.pesticide: Myclobutanil (Systhane) at 1ml per litre OR Triadimefon (Bayleton) at 0.5g per litre
treatment.application: Apply fungicide from pink bud stage through 3 weeks after petal fall.
treatment.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Remove juniper hosts within 3km radius if feasible.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed. Continue regular maintenance.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue preventive spray schedule during growing season.
treatment.prevention: Maintain proper nutrition, irrigation, and pruning schedule.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Maintain soil pH between 4.5-5.5.
treatment.prevention: Ensure adequate mulching and proper drainage.

Cherry Powdery Mildew.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, leaf curling, stunted growth

treatment.immediate_action: Remove severely infected shoots. Improve air circulation by pruning.
treatment.pesticide: Sulphur 80WP (Sulfex) at 3g per litre OR Hexaconazole 5EC (Contaf) at 1ml per litre
treatment.application: Spray at first sign of infection. Repeat every 10-14 days.
treatment.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation. Ensure proper plant spacing.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular care.
treatment.prevention: Maintain balanced fertilization and proper irrigation.

Corn Cercospora Leaf Spot.symptoms: Small rectangular tan to grey lesions between veins, lesions may coalesce

treatment.immediate_action: Remove lower infected leaves to reduce spore load.
treatment.pesticide: Propiconazole 25EC (Tilt) at 1ml per litre OR Mancozeb 75WP at 2.5g per litre
treatment.application: Apply at first sign of infection. Repeat at 14-day intervals.
treatment.prevention: Rotate crops with non-host plants. Use resistant hybrids. Manage plant debris.

Corn Common Rust.symptoms: Small circular to elongate brown pustules on both leaf surfaces

treatment.immediate_action: Monitor pustule density. Treat if >50% of ear leaf area is affected before tasseling.
treatment.pesticide: Propiconazole 25EC (Tilt) at 1ml per litre OR Mancozeb 75WP at 2.5g per litre
treatment.application: Apply preventively when rust first appears. One or two applications usually sufficient.
treatment.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Early planting reduces rust severity.

Corn Northern Leaf Blight.symptoms: Long elliptical greyish-green to tan lesions 3-15cm, starting from lower leaves

treatment.immediate_action: No immediate cure for existing lesions. Focus on protecting upper canopy.
treatment.pesticide: Propiconazole 25EC at 1ml per litre OR Azoxystrobin 23SC (Amistar) at 1ml per litre
treatment.application: Apply at tasseling if disease is present on third leaf below ear or higher.
treatment.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate with soybean or other non-host. Incorporate crop residue.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular nutrient management.
treatment.prevention: Maintain proper plant population and balanced fertilization.

Grape Black Rot.symptoms: Tan circular leaf lesions with dark borders, fruit shrivels to hard black mummies

treatment.immediate_action: Remove and destroy all mummified berries and infected plant parts.
treatment.pesticide: Mancozeb 75WP at 2.5g per litre OR Myclobutanil at 1ml per litre
treatment.application: Start spraying from bud break. Critical period is pre-bloom through 4 weeks post-bloom.
treatment.prevention: Remove mummies during winter. Maintain open canopy for air circulation.

Grape Esca (Black Measles).symptoms: Tiger-stripe pattern on leaves, dark spots on berries, sudden vine collapse in severe cases

treatment.immediate_action: No effective chemical cure exists. Remove severely affected vines to prevent spread.
treatment.pesticide: Sodium arsenite is banned. Trichoderma-based biocontrol (Trichoderma viride at 5g per litre) can help as preventive.
treatment.application: Apply Trichoderma to pruning wounds immediately after pruning.
treatment.prevention: Minimize large pruning wounds. Protect cuts with wound sealant. Avoid stress conditions.

Grape Leaf Blight.symptoms: Brown irregular lesions on leaf margins, may progress inward, premature leaf drop

treatment.immediate_action: Remove infected leaves. Improve canopy management for air flow.
treatment.pesticide: Mancozeb 75WP at 2.5g per litre OR Copper oxychloride at 3g per litre
treatment.application: Spray every 10-14 days during wet weather.
treatment.prevention: Proper canopy management. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove fallen debris.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue preventive fungicide schedule during monsoon.
treatment.prevention: Maintain proper training system and canopy management.

Orange Citrus Greening.symptoms: Asymmetric yellowing of leaves (blotchy mottle), lopsided bitter fruit, green coloring remains on fruit

treatment.immediate_action: This is a bacterial disease (HLB) with NO CURE. Remove and destroy infected trees to prevent spread.
treatment.pesticide: Control the vector Asian citrus psyllid with Imidacloprid 17.8SL (Confidor) at 0.5ml per litre.
treatment.application: Spray insecticide when psyllid population is detected. Apply nutritional sprays (zinc, manganese) to prolong tree life.
treatment.prevention: Use certified disease-free nursery stock only. Monitor for psyllids regularly. Remove infected trees immediately.

Peach Bacterial Spot.symptoms: Small dark water-soaked spots on leaves that become angular, fruit spots with cracks

treatment.immediate_action: Remove severely infected branches. Reduce overhead irrigation.
treatment.pesticide: Copper hydroxide (Kocide) at 2g per litre. Apply oxytetracycline (Streptocycline) at 0.5g per litre.
treatment.application: Apply copper at petal fall. Repeat every 10-14 days during wet weather.
treatment.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain proper spacing.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular care.
treatment.prevention: Apply dormant copper spray in winter.

Bell Pepper Bacterial Spot.symptoms: Small dark raised spots on leaves, water-soaked lesions on fruit, leaf yellowing and drop

treatment.immediate_action: Remove infected plant parts. Avoid working in wet fields to prevent spread.
treatment.pesticide: Copper oxychloride (Blitox) at 3g per litre + Streptocycline at 0.5g per litre
treatment.application: Spray at first sign. Repeat every 7-10 days. Alternate with Mancozeb.
treatment.prevention: Use disease-free seed. Rotate crops. Avoid overhead irrigation.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue balanced fertilization.
treatment.prevention: Maintain proper spacing and staking for air circulation.

Potato Early Blight.symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring lesions (target board pattern) on lower older leaves first

treatment.immediate_action: Remove and destroy lower infected leaves. Do not compost.
treatment.pesticide: Mancozeb 75WP (Dithane M-45) at 2.5g per litre OR Chlorothalonil 75WP at 2g per litre
treatment.application: Start spraying when first lesions appear. Repeat every 7-10 days. Alternate fungicides.
treatment.prevention: Use certified seed tubers. Maintain adequate potassium fertilization. Avoid water stress.

Potato Late Blight.symptoms: Water-soaked dark green to brown lesions on leaves, white fuzzy growth on leaf undersides in humid conditions, rapid plant collapse

treatment.immediate_action: This is an EMERGENCY disease. Remove and destroy all infected plants immediately. Isolate the field.
treatment.pesticide: Cymoxanil + Mancozeb (Curzate M8) at 3g per litre OR Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) at 2.5g per litre
treatment.application: Apply immediately upon detection. Spray every 5-7 days in wet weather. Use systemic fungicides.
treatment.prevention: Use resistant varieties (Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Girdhari). Avoid late planting. Ensure good drainage. Destroy volunteer plants.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular hilling and irrigation schedule.
treatment.prevention: Use certified seed. Monitor regularly during monsoon season.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular pruning and training.
treatment.prevention: Remove old canes after fruiting. Maintain proper spacing.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular crop management.
treatment.prevention: Use treated seed. Rotate with cereals.

Squash Powdery Mildew.symptoms: White powdery patches on upper leaf surface, yellowing, premature leaf death

treatment.immediate_action: Remove severely infected leaves. Improve air circulation.
treatment.pesticide: Sulphur 80WP at 3g per litre OR Hexaconazole 5EC (Contaf) at 1ml per litre OR neem oil 2%
treatment.application: Spray at first sign. Repeat every 7-10 days. Avoid sulphur in temperatures above 35°C.
treatment.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid dense planting. Water at base, not overhead.

Strawberry Leaf Scorch.symptoms: Small dark purple spots on upper leaf surface, spots enlarge and merge, leaf margins dry and curl

treatment.immediate_action: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Thin plants for better air flow.
treatment.pesticide: Copper oxychloride at 3g per litre OR Captan 50WP at 2g per litre
treatment.application: Apply fungicide at renovation and again in fall. Spray during wet spring weather.
treatment.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Renovate beds annually. Remove old leaf debris.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed.
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular runner management.
treatment.prevention: Maintain proper mulching and avoid waterlogging.

Tomato Bacterial Spot.symptoms: Small dark raised spots on leaves and fruit, leaf yellowing and defoliation, scabby fruit spots

treatment.immediate_action: Remove infected plant parts. Avoid working with wet plants.
treatment.pesticide: Copper oxychloride (Blitox) at 3g per litre + Streptocycline at 0.5g per litre
treatment.application: Spray at first symptom. Repeat every 7-10 days.
treatment.prevention: Use disease-free seedlings. Rotate crops for 2-3 years. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Tomato Early Blight.symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring spots (target board pattern) on older lower leaves first, yellowing around spots

treatment.immediate_action: Remove infected lower leaves immediately. Stake plants for better air flow.
treatment.pesticide: Mancozeb 75WP (Dithane M-45) at 2.5g per litre OR Chlorothalonil 75WP at 2g per litre
treatment.application: Start at transplanting. Spray every 7-10 days. Alternate between contact and systemic fungicides.
treatment.prevention: Mulch around plants. Avoid overhead watering. Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 3 years.

Tomato Late Blight.symptoms: Large dark water-soaked lesions on leaves and stems, white fuzzy growth underneath in humid conditions, rapid wilting

treatment.immediate_action: EMERGENCY: Remove and destroy all infected plants immediately. Do not compost. Alert neighbouring farms.
treatment.pesticide: Cymoxanil + Mancozeb (Curzate M8) at 3g per litre OR Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) at 2.5g per litre
treatment.application: Apply immediately. Spray every 5 days during active outbreak. Use systemic fungicide.
treatment.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Avoid late-season planting. Destroy all crop debris after harvest.

Tomato Leaf Mold.symptoms: Pale yellow spots on upper leaf surface, olive-green to brown velvety growth on undersides

treatment.immediate_action: Improve ventilation in greenhouse. Remove lower infected leaves.
treatment.pesticide: Mancozeb 75WP at 2.5g per litre OR Chlorothalonil at 2g per litre
treatment.application: Spray at first sign. Improve greenhouse ventilation immediately.
treatment.prevention: Reduce humidity below 85%. Increase plant spacing. Use drip irrigation only.

Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot.symptoms: Many small circular spots with dark borders and grey centres, tiny black dots (pycnidia) in centre of spots

treatment.immediate_action: Remove and destroy infected lower leaves up to first fruit cluster.
treatment.pesticide: Mancozeb 75WP at 2.5g per litre OR Copper oxychloride at 3g per litre
treatment.application: Start at first symptom. Spray every 7-10 days throughout season.
treatment.prevention: Mulch to prevent soil splash. Stake plants. Rotate crops for 3 years.

Tomato Spider Mites.symptoms: Fine stippling (tiny yellow dots) on leaves, fine webbing on undersides, leaf bronzing and drying

treatment.immediate_action: Spray plants with strong water jet to dislodge mites. Remove severely infested leaves.
treatment.pesticide: Abamectin 1.8EC (Vertimec) at 0.5ml per litre OR Spiromesifen (Oberon) at 0.5ml per litre OR neem oil 2%
treatment.application: Apply miticide on leaf undersides. Repeat every 5-7 days for 2-3 applications.
treatment.prevention: Avoid dusty conditions. Maintain adequate irrigation. Use predatory mites (Phytoseiulus) as biocontrol.

Tomato Target Spot.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings on leaves, larger and more irregular than early blight, affects all plant parts

treatment.immediate_action: Remove infected leaves. Improve staking and plant spacing.
treatment.pesticide: Azoxystrobin 23SC (Amistar) at 1ml per litre OR Chlorothalonil 75WP at 2g per litre
treatment.application: Start preventive sprays early in season. Spray every 7-14 days.
treatment.prevention: Ensure good air circulation. Rotate crops. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization.

Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus.symptoms: Severe upward curling of leaves, yellowing of leaf margins, stunted growth, flower drop, NO fruit production

treatment.immediate_action: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Control whitefly vector urgently.
treatment.pesticide: For whitefly control: Imidacloprid 17.8SL (Confidor) at 0.5ml per litre OR Thiamethoxam 25WG (Actara) at 0.5g per litre
treatment.application: Apply systemic insecticide as drench at transplanting + foliar spray every 10 days. Use yellow sticky traps.
treatment.prevention: Use virus-resistant varieties (ToLCV resistant). Use whitefly-proof net houses. Maintain crop-free period of 2 months.

Tomato Mosaic Virus.symptoms: Light and dark green mottled pattern on leaves, leaf distortion and fern-like appearance, reduced fruit size

treatment.immediate_action: Remove infected plants. Wash hands and tools with milk solution (20%) before touching healthy plants.
treatment.pesticide: No chemical treatment for virus. Control aphid vectors with Imidacloprid 17.8SL at 0.5ml per litre.
treatment.application: Focus on vector control and sanitation. Spray neem oil 2% as insect deterrent.
treatment.prevention: Use virus-free certified seed. Disinfect tools between plants. Do not use tobacco products while handling plants.

treatment.immediate_action: No treatment needed. Your tomato plant looks great!
treatment.pesticide: None required
treatment.application: Continue regular staking, pruning of suckers, and balanced fertilization.
treatment.prevention: Monitor weekly for early signs of disease. Maintain proper spacing and hygiene.
